reforms.description: The government adopted accrual accounting techniques for the 1998 budget to promote more efficient financial management practices, which included changes in how revenues and expenditures are reported.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved transparency in budgeting can lead to better resource allocation and financial management, supporting overall economic efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The government presented its 1998 budget more transparently, using accrual accounting techniques.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform enhances the clarity and accountability of public finances, which is significant for long-term fiscal health.
reforms.source_quote: The government presented its 1998 budget more transparently, using accrual accounting techniques, with the objective of promoting more efficient and effective financial management practices in central government.

reforms.description: The Icelandic government implemented a three-year tax reform programme aimed at reducing disincentives for investment and work, which included a reduction in the standard rate of income tax and changes to capital income taxation. Additionally, the Parliament passed laws to restructure government-owned financial institutions, transforming state banks into corporations and creating the Icelandic Investment Bank to consolidate various industrial investment credit funds.
reforms.component_name: Tax Reform and Financial Institution Restructuring
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to enhance investment incentives and improve the efficiency of financial institutions, which are expected to support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms were enacted and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change in the tax and financial system.
reforms.source_quote: In the 1997 budget, the first stage of a three-year reform of the tax system was implemented, covering the taxation of income from employment, pensions and capital as well as the payroll tax.

reforms.description: The New Business Venture Fund was established to provide start-up and seed capital for new businesses, particularly in rural areas, with an initial asset value of IKr 4 billion and additional government capital of IKr 1 billion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to stimulate entrepreneurship and support the growth of new businesses, which can enhance economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the fund is a significant initiative to support start-ups, though it is not as transformative as the tax reform.
reforms.source_quote: Another institution – the New Business Venture Fund – was also created by removing parts of their existing activities from the four above-mentioned funds before they were merged into the IIB.
